steps.title: Compare failure and flexibility
steps.content: The passage says ordinary aerogels are brittle and can fracture after heat exposure. Patel's new aerogel is flexible, contracts when heated, and recovers after major compression, so the logical inference is that it avoids the weakness that makes other aerogels break down.

steps.title: Reject unsupported inferences
steps.content: Choice A invents a reason based on air-pocket proportion. Choice B claims lower heat resistance, which conflicts with the positive evidence. Choice C introduces gradual versus rapid temperature shifts, a distinction the passage never discusses. The answer is D.
